user.title: Connect to ChronoSnap
user.description: Enter the URL and API key for your ChronoSnap server.

data_description.instance_name: A friendly name for this ChronoSnap instance (useful when connecting to multiple servers)
data_description.url: The base URL of your ChronoSnap instance (e.g., http://192.168.1.100:5000)
data_description.api_key: Found in ChronoSnap Settings → API Key

error.cannot_connect: Unable to connect to ChronoSnap server. Check the URL and ensure the server is running.
error.invalid_auth: Invalid API key. Check your API key in ChronoSnap Settings.

abort.already_configured: This ChronoSnap server is already configured.

init.title: Timelapse Profiles
init.description: Manage your timelapse profiles. Each profile watches an entity and automatically creates timelapses.

data.action: What would you like to do?

profile_basic.title: Profile Setup
profile_basic.description: Configure the stream source and trigger entity for this profile.

data_description.name: A friendly name for this timelapse profile
data_description.stream_url: The RTSP, HTTP, or device URL for the camera
data_description.stream_type: The protocol type of the camera stream
data_description.trigger_entity: The entity to watch for state changes
data_description.active_state: Capture starts when the entity enters this state and stops when it leaves
data_description.exclude_states: Comma-separated states that should NOT trigger a stop. If the entity enters one of these states, capturing continues.
data_description.start_delay_seconds: Seconds to wait before creating the job after the entity enters the active state. If the entity leaves the active state during this delay, the job is not created. Set to 0 for no delay.
data_description.debounce_seconds: Seconds to wait before stopping after the entity leaves the active state (prevents false triggers)

profile_capture.title: Capture & Video Settings
profile_capture.description: Configure how frames are captured and how the timelapse video is built.

data.auto_cleanup: Auto-delete job after video is built

data_description.interval_mode: Fixed: capture at a set interval. Target duration: calculate interval to produce a specific video length.
data_description.interval_seconds: Seconds between captures (minimum 10). Only used in fixed mode.
data_description.fps: Frames per second for the output timelapse video
data_description.quality: Encoding quality for the output video
data_description.capture_quality: Image quality for each captured frame
data_description.auto_cleanup: When enabled, the capture job and its raw frames are automatically deleted after the video is built. The video itself is always preserved.
data_description.tag_ids: ChronoSnap tags to apply to the job and video

profile_target.title: Target Duration Settings
profile_target.description: The capture interval will be calculated so the final video is approximately the target duration. Formula: entity_value ÷ (target_duration × fps), minimum 10 seconds.

data_description.target_duration: Desired length of the final timelapse video in seconds
data_description.duration_entity: Entity whose state contains either the remaining duration in seconds or an estimated end/finish time
